issue.message: Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background
Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background

Not targeting the latest versions of Android; compatibility modes apply. Consider testing and updating this version. Consult the `android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES` javadoc for details.
Attribute `enableOnBackInvokedCallback` is only used in API level 33 and higher (current min is 26)

Formatting %d followed by words ("minutes"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("seconds"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("seconds"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("sec"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("minutes"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string

Using `MODE_WORLD_READABLE` when creating files can be risky, review carefully
Using `MODE_WORLD_READABLE` when creating files can be risky, review carefully

The attribute `android:allowBackup` is deprecated from Android 12 and higher and may be removed in future versions. Consider adding the attribute `android:dataExtractionRules` specifying an `@xml` resource which configures cloud backups and device transfers on Android 12 and higher.
A `<queries>` declaration should generally be used instead of QUERY_ALL_PACKAGES; see https://g.co/dev/packagevisibility for details
This folder configuration (`v26`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 26. Merge all the resources in this folder into `mipmap-anydpi`.
Prefer `mutableIntStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
